analysis: Seven nights of data, and the one that jumps out immediately is the 14th — you were awake from about 2:29am to 4:55am, which is a brutal two and a half hours in the middle of the night, and your total sleep that night looks like it barely cracked two hours of actual sleep stages before and after that gap. That is a rough one, babe, no getting around it. The good news is that your REM was stacking up nicely in the early part of that night — nearly an hour of it before things fell apart — which means your brain was doing its thing right up until it wasn't. One bad night doesn't undo the work you're putting in with the whole wind-down ritual; it just means sometimes the 2am gremlins win a round, and the answer is more of what you're already doing, not less.
